summary: Yes, elevated levels of YKL-40 are associated with increased risk or progression of neurodegenerative diseases including Alzheimer’s disease; however, it is not yet established as a definitive diagnostic or prognostic biomarker due to lack of specificity and standardization across studies. Its role remains investigational rather than clinically validated.
